title: Rental income not included in lodged tax return letter
description: What to do if you get a data matching letter about needing to include rental income in your tax return.
keywords: Data matching letter, rental income, omitted income, letter, rental

content_blocks.text: We may send you a letter if our data suggests you have rental income that hasn't been reported in your tax return.
We collect data from third parties, including:
If you receive one of these letters, it means you should check your records to confirm whether you have included all rental income received from the properties stated in the letter.
If all income has been correctly reported for the years indicated, you don’t need to reply to the letter.
If you need to amend your tax return
You can amend past tax returns by:

items: asking your registered tax agent to lodge the amendment on your behalf
using our online services through myGov and include all rental income.

content_text:
## Receiving your letter
We may send you a letter if our data suggests you have rental income that hasn't been reported in your tax return.
We collect data from third parties, including:
- financial institutions
- property managers
- land title offices
- sharing economy platforms
- bond authorities
- insurers.
## What you need to do
If you receive one of these letters, it means you should check your records to confirm whether you have included all rental income received from the properties stated in the letter.
If all income has been correctly reported for the years indicated, you don’t need to reply to the letter.
### If you need to amend your tax return
You can amend past tax returns by:
- asking your registered tax agent to lodge the amendment on your behalf
- using our online services through myGov and include all rental income.
